leagueBriefing: LSU found its rhythm at the plate just in time to salvage a game in the series against Texas A&M, using a late-inning offensive surge and a complete game from Tatum Clopton to avoid the home sweep.

games.headline: Clopton dominates as LSU avoids sweep with dominant win over Texas A&M
games.brief: LSU snapped its conference skid in a big way Monday night, riding a career-defining performance from Tatum Clopton to a 7-2 win over No. 16 Texas A&M. After dropping the first two games of the series, the Tigers finally broke through with a four-run sixth inning that turned a tight contest into a comfortable finale. Clopton was the story in the circle, tossing a complete game and matching her season high with six strikeouts to keep the Aggies' potent lineup at bay while LSU's bats tallied eight hits to ensure they didn't leave the series empty-handed.
bullets: Tatum Clopton earned her first complete game of the season, allowing just one earned run and striking out six over seven innings.
Alix Franklin ignited the Tiger offense with a solo home run in the second inning to give LSU its first lead of the entire series.
A late four-run sixth inning for LSU featured RBI walks from Avery Hodge and Jalia Lassiter followed by a two-run double from Maci Bergeron.
Texas A&M's Kennedy Powell continued her hot streak, going 2-for-4 with a double and scoring both of the Aggies' runs.
The Aggies committed two fielding errors and surrendered five walks, contributing to LSU's largest scoring output of the series.
games.context: The victory marked LSU's first SEC win of the season and prevented a series sweep at the hands of the Aggies.
